title: Hylocereus extensus
taxonomy: Hylocereus extensus is classified as a species of cactus, belonging to the genus Hylocereus. This genus is part of the family Cactaceae, which comprises a wide variety of cacti species. Hylocereus extensus is native to Central and South America, where it grows as an epiphyte or a terrestrial plant. It is known for its unique, funnel-shaped flowers that bloom at night and wilt by dawn. The species is also referred to as a "Queen of the Night" due to its nocturnal flowering habit.
